stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Stomata are tiny pores on the surface of plant leaves.
  • Stomata allow gases such as carbon dioxide and oxygen to enter and exit the leaf.
  • Members of the same plant species typically have similar stomatal densities.
  • The common bean (Phaseolus vulgaris) and the soybean (Glycine max) are species of legumes.
  • The common bean has roughly 280 stomata per square millimeter on its lower leaf surface.
  • The soybean has roughly 150 stomata per square millimeter on its lower leaf surface.
stem: The student wants to specify how many stomata per square millimeter the common bean has on its lower leaf surface. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: The common bean (Phaseolus vulgaris) has roughly 280 stomata per square millimeter on its lower leaf surface.
The common bean's stomata are tiny pores that allow gases to enter and exit the leaf.
The common bean (Phaseolus vulgaris) and the soybean (Glycine max) both have stomata, but the common bean has more per square millimeter than the soybean does.
The soybean, a species of legume, has roughly 150 stomata per square millimeter on its lower leaf surface.

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The sentence directly specifies how many stomata per square millimeter the common bean has on its lower leaf surface, as indicated in the notes: roughly 280.

Choice B is incorrect. While the sentence mentions the common bean's stomata, it does not specify how many it has per square millimeter. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence indicates that the common bean has more stomata per square millimeter than the soybean; it does not specify the exact number. Choice D is incorrect. The sentence specifies the stomatal density of the soybean, not the common bean.
